title: cyclophyllum valetonianum
intro: Cyclophyllum valetonianum, also known as the Koa haole, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Cyclophyllum valetonianum include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark is often made into a tea or decoction to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
